page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 124.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and does not include any GPU accelerators.

The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8). In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2250.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 35.04 GB/sec. Multi-core performance falls into the average range, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13151.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 13917.69.

Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1369.22 GB/sec and an uncached large block write speed of 50.44 GB/sec. For database operations, the server achieves a Passmark database score of 8434.49 and handles 24.3 million Redis SET operations per second. LLM inference benchmarks show strong text generation speeds of 317.01 tokens/sec for small 135M models, though performance scales down to 13.49 tokens/sec for medium 7B models.

The absence of local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated GPU hardware limits this instance to workloads reliant on network-attached storage and CPU-bound processing. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-threaded performance, the server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text processing, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 124.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2250.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 35.04 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally align with mid-range averages, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 13151.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 24.3 million operations per second. This hardware configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, text processing, and CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 124 GB RAM, and no local storage. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-16 is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 124 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz.
124.0 GB of RAM providing a high 7.75 GB per core allocation.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, and text processing.
